rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of And Motion To File First Amended Unlawful Detainer Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 22, 2020 line 9. PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION's Motion To File First Amended Unlawful Detainer Complaint GRANTED. Plaintiff shall have leave to add an additional Plaintiff. Plaintiff shall allege facts to show standing to recover possession in their own name for each Plaintiff claiming such right at the time of the filing of the Amended Complaint. Trial date is vacated. Discovery remains open for Defendant to conduct discovery into the new allegations of the Amended Complaint. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Action From A Limited Jurisdiction Action To An Unlimited Jurisdiction Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 22, 2020 line 9. PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION's Motion To Reclassify Action From A Limited Jurisdiction Action To An Unlimited Jurisdiction Action Hearing Required to address timeliness. Jurisdictional limit reached almost a year ago in October 2019. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCINE VERDIER VS. TONY SINGH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 22, 2020 line 10. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly identify in paragraph 1 of the Form Complaint the capacity they bring this action. Plaintiff appears to be alleging two distinct causes of action seeking possession based on the state of facts to be discovered, i.e. termination of license and termination of tenancy. Plaintiff shall clearly allege each. Combining these grounds for termination in one cause of action alleged in paragraph 6 renders the complaint uncertain. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCINE VERDIER VS. TONY SINGH ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Sep 22, 2020 line 10. Defendant's Joinder To Demurrer SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly identify in paragraph 1 of the Form Complaint the capacity they bring this action. Plaintiff appears to be alleging two distinct causes of action seeking possession based on the state of facts to be discovered, i.e. termination of license and termination of tenancy. Plaintiff shall clearly allege each. Combining these grounds for termination in one cause of action alleged in paragraph 6 renders the complaint uncertain. =(501/CFH)
